query: TimescaleDB ClickHouse real-time analytics 50000 events per second
answer: TimescaleDB and ClickHouse both support real-time analytics, but ClickHouse excels in high ingestion speeds and low query latency. TimescaleDB is PostgreSQL-compatible and handles high-cardinality time-series data effectively.

results.title: What is Real-Time Analytics? A Complete Guide (2026) | Engineering | ClickHouse Resource Hub
results.snippet: At the heart of any real-time analytics system is a database that ingests data quickly and serves low-latency queries at scale. Real-time databases must ingest hundreds of thousands or millions of records per second and immediately make this data available to queries, providing users with access to fresh data. ClickHouse Cloud is a real-time data analytics platform. ClickHouse has the characteristics needed in a real-time analytics system, described earlier in this article - ingestion speed, query latency, and concurrency. A real-time analytics system must have three key characteristics: high ingestion speed to handle streaming data, low query latency (sub-second) to provide instant insights, and high concurrency to support many simultaneous users, which is common in user-facing applications. Modern real-time analytical databases like ClickHouse use a familiar SQL interface, allowing developers to write complex queries on streaming data to extract insights without learning a new language. Real-time analytics works by continuously ingesting data from event streams (like Kafka) into a specialized database.

results.title: ClickHouse® vs TimescaleDB: Best for real-time analytics 2026
results.snippet: Modern ClickHouse® workflows address this by combining **high-throughput initial loads**, **low-latency change data capture**, and **automatic schema synchronization** for both one-time and continuous migration scenarios. This approach minimizes risk, provides an early validation of query performance, and allows teams to phase out legacy infrastructure progressively.Because ClickHouse® can ingest and query replicated data in parallel, it becomes a **seamless analytical companion** to time-series systems during transition periods. By **including all child tables in the logical replication publication**, the migration engine ensures that every insert, update, or delete from any chunk is streamed correctly to the target ClickHouse® table.The ingestion process reconstructs these chunks into a **single continuous table** in ClickHouse®, ordered by timestamp and optimized for columnar storage.This approach retains the same time-series semantics while benefiting from ClickHouse®’s **compression and vectorized execution** for analytical workloads. * **For relational time-series integration:** Choose TimescaleDB when you combine time-series data with traditional relational tables using joins, or when your team's PostgreSQL expertise outweighs the performance benefits of ClickHouse®.

results.title: ClickHouse vs TimescaleDB vs InfluxDB: 2026 Benchmarks & Comparison | sanj.dev
results.snippet: Data flows from sensors to **InfluxDB** for real-time alerting (sub-millisecond response), then to **Kafka**, which ships it to **ClickHouse** for historical analytics (years of data), while **TimescaleDB** handles transactional control systems. According to 2026 benchmarks, ClickHouse typically achieves 15-30x compression for time-series data, while TimescaleDB achieves 10-15x. ### Can TimescaleDB handle high-cardinality time-series data? Yes. Most successful deployments in 2026 use a hybrid approach: InfluxDB for real-time alerting, ClickHouse for historical analytics, and TimescaleDB for transactional control systems with PostgreSQL compatibility. Time-series databases are fundamental infrastructure for trading systems, market data platforms, and financial analytics. *Hot Data (Trading):* Use **InfluxDB** or **TimescaleDB** for real-time market data capture and sub-second queries. **Real-World Stack:** A typical trading platform uses **TimescaleDB** for real-time tick capture and order management, **InfluxDB** for monitoring and metrics, and **ClickHouse** for backtesting, analytics, and compliance reporting. Choosing between ClickHouse, TimescaleDB, kdb+, and other time-series databases has long-term implications for your fintech’s performance and cost.
